authority_profile: Curated incident database with strong discovery and comparison value, but not a final authority for legal status or allegation truth.
access_method: Public cite pages, public snapshot surfaces, and bounded route inspection.
reuse_constraints: Do not mirror weekly snapshots or automate bulk pulls until snapshot semantics and rate/terms posture are documented.
language_scope: English-facing interface with global incident coverage.
likely_case_types: Public incident reports, deployment failures, misuse, and controversy traces.
saturation_metric: 1 stable public incident page sample plus one appendix row verified; public backup discoverability confirmed; bulk/API semantics still partial.
local_seed_evidence: Stable public cite-page sample with visible incident ID, date, and report-count labels.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for overlap, residual-gap detection, and source-baseline discussion; weak alone for legal/compliance or incident-truth claims.
next_verification_move: Verify one snapshot manifest or single-record machine-readable extraction that ties public page labels to field names without broad harvesting.
dashboard_hook: Source-family saturation map; comparator-overlap panel; interoperability readiness board.
provenance_note: Grounded in bounded 2026-06-07 local verification of public cite-page and snapshot surfaces; GraphQL POST remained origin-restricted.

authority_profile: Official institutional monitor with strong policy-facing authority for incident indexing and controlled vocabularies, but not itself a final authority on underlying allegations.
access_method: Public incident pages and unauthenticated JSON facet endpoints under the OECD incidents service.
reuse_constraints: Keep use metadata-first and citation-oriented until list/detail discovery mechanics and rights posture are documented more fully.
language_scope: English-facing interface with international article references and platform vocabularies.
likely_case_types: Public incident summaries, article-linked records, and policy-facing monitoring rows.
saturation_metric: 1 stable public detail-page sample plus live country/language/concept endpoint evidence verified locally; record-discovery route still partial.
local_seed_evidence: Stable slugged public detail page with embedded structured JSON and live facet endpoints.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for comparator metadata, vocabulary bridges, and country/language context; still insufficient alone for strong factual or legal claims.
next_verification_move: Trace one public detail page back to a stable list/discovery route and document the minimal safe metadata subset.
dashboard_hook: Country/language coverage comparison; vocabulary bridge; incident-monitor interoperability board.
provenance_note: Grounded in 2026-06-07 local detail-page and facet-endpoint checks; page footer still signals OECD rights reservation.

authority_profile: High structured technical authority for public AI vulnerability/report metadata when exact IDs and references are preserved.
access_method: Public website plus openly downloadable GitHub raw JSON and repository contents.
reuse_constraints: MIT-licensed structured metadata lowers reuse friction, but import should stay bounded to IDs, dates, metrics, references, and linked report/vulnerability objects.
likely_case_types: AI vulnerabilities, security edge cases, technical misuse, and failure reports.
saturation_metric: 9 scanned security rows carry 1 canonical direct AVID join plus 8 explicit placeholder/context-only rows in the reviewer-safe appendix.
local_seed_evidence: Canonical direct local security-row join verified for LIMEN-000002; placeholder/context-only rows remain explicit rather than overstated as solved links.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for technical mechanism, vulnerability metadata, and one direct security-row bridge; weak for general incident census or treating placeholder rows as solved joins.
next_verification_move: Treat the current AVID appendix as the reviewer-safe control surface; only add a second canonical direct join or exact negative-match rule if justified, otherwise return effort to sf08/sf09 authority hardening.
dashboard_hook: Security-edge-case bridge; direct-versus-placeholder join panel; reviewer-safe interoperability appendix.
provenance_note: Grounded in 2026-06-07 raw GitHub JSON verification and the live join-prototype/control pass.

authority_profile: High structured authority for adversarial-ML and agentic-security techniques, mitigations, and case-study relations.
access_method: Public website plus Apache-licensed GitHub YAML/data distribution.
reuse_constraints: Apache-2.0 lowers reuse friction for technique/mitigation/case-study metadata; do not overstate case studies as exhaustive incident coverage.
likely_case_types: Adversarial techniques, mitigations, case-study relations, and agentic/security anomaly structure.
saturation_metric: ATLAS technique context appears across all 9 current security rows with 12 technique-ID occurrences; the appendix separates generalized case-context, no-direct-case-study, and cautious-context-only states.
local_seed_evidence: Local security rows already use ATLAS technique context broadly, but exact case-study equivalence remains limited.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for security-technique structure, mitigation framing, and operational technique-context joins; weak for non-security completeness or direct case-study equivalence claims.
next_verification_move: Treat the current ATLAS appendix as the reviewer-safe control surface; only add a defensible exact case-study ID or stronger independent anchor for cautious rows if justified, otherwise return effort to sf08/sf09 authority hardening.
dashboard_hook: Security/agentic crosswalk; technique-coverage heatmap; case-context-versus-cautious-context panel.
provenance_note: Grounded in bounded local parsing of the public ATLAS distributions and the current security crosswalk package.

authority_profile: Secondary but important structured authority for risk and harm definitions; not an incident-truth source.
reuse_constraints: Use as attributed taxonomy/definition layer only; bulk export route not yet verified locally.
likely_case_types: Risk classes, hazard definitions, and residual-category comparison support.
saturation_metric: Homepage-level reuse signal and taxonomy role verified; no bulk export route yet documented.
local_seed_evidence: Local crosswalk package already uses MIT AI Risk as a definition/taxonomy comparator rather than a case source.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for methods, taxonomy, and harm-definition claims; invalid as event evidence.
next_verification_move: Extract one compact manual risk-category bridge for the most used LIMEN harm labels before any stronger comparative language.
dashboard_hook: Taxonomy heatmap; residual-category overlay; methods table on definition-layer comparators.
provenance_note: Grounded in 2026-06-07 homepage-level verification including a visible CC BY 4.0 publishing-principles signal.

authority_profile: Secondary conceptual authority for harm framing and related-work comparison, but not machine-ready incident evidence.
access_method: Public Georgetown CSET article surface plus exact public PDF/README anchors captured in local bridge work.
reuse_constraints: Use citation/manual mapping only; no explicit open machine-reuse grant or row-level public dataset route was verified locally.
likely_case_types: Harm framing, interpretive taxonomy support, and related-work positioning.
saturation_metric: Public article plus exact PDF/README anchors verified; 7-row local harm bridge and 5-rule coding memo exist; machine-ingest path remains absent.
local_seed_evidence: Local CSET bridge and coding-rule artifacts support an appendix-only concept-bridge family rather than a queue placeholder.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for appendix-only harm-posture interpretation and related-work positioning; invalid as event evidence or ingest-ready comparator.
next_verification_move: Keep the family explicitly outside machine-ingest claims and add a second low-drift reviewer pass before any row-level posture aggregate is exposed.

authority_profile: High authority for official actions, allegations, orders, fines, guidance, and procedural posture within issuing jurisdiction.
access_method: Official regulator portals, enforcement pages, press releases, decisions, and linked official documents.
reuse_constraints: Public-document use only; preserve original-language nuance and narrow posture-based wording.
likely_case_types: Enforcement actions, deceptive-marketing cases, privacy breaches, official warnings, and procedural escalations.
saturation_metric: 7 authoritative case packages are captured locally across Italy, the United States, and the United Kingdom, including two settled SEC administrative-order exemplars.
local_seed_evidence: Hashed regulator-source rows and direct order/PDF follow-ups now include Italian DPA order text, ICO PDF-backed biometric cases, EEOC summary-level escalation, DOJ charging-stage-plus-indictment, and SEC administrative orders.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Best current family for upgrading weak media or company claims into paper-grade bounded statements.
next_verification_move: Add one non-U.S./non-UK follow-through row tied to a public record or court-facing document to reduce current geography concentration, while keeping the court-family bottleneck on exact sf08 depth separate.
dashboard_hook: Authority-weighted evidence matrix; regulator-action timeline; jurisdiction map.
provenance_note: Grounded in local authoritative ledger rows with explicit posture and provenance controls.

authority_profile: Highest authority for filings, judgments, consent decrees, and procedural posture when records are official and public.
access_method: Public court databases, judgment portals, tribunal repositories, or official DOJ/agency-linked docket materials.
reuse_constraints: Respect access limits; no scraping behind logins, paywalls, sealed, or private attachments.
likely_case_types: Litigation, injunctions, complaints, decrees, judgments, and review of automated/public AI decisions.
saturation_metric: 1 DOJ-linked indictment PDF captured locally with partial caption OCR plus 1 EEOC filing/settlement pair exposing Civil Action No. 1:22-cv-02565 in agency summaries but still no open court-originating complaint, decree, docket entry, or judicial order text.
local_seed_evidence: A DOJ-hosted indictment PDF gives one official U.S. charging-document exemplar, while the iTutorGroup row still lacks captured court-originating text.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Critical for hostile-reviewer resilience where regulator or agency summaries alone are too thin.
next_verification_move: Either capture one open complaint, consent decree, docket entry, or judicial order text for the iTutorGroup row from a genuinely different public court-facing surface or clearly labeled open mirror, or use a materially different OCR stack / another public exact-caption source before retrying the Nate suffix.
dashboard_hook: Regulator-to-court escalation matrix; procedural-posture timeline; Route-B blocker panel.
provenance_note: Grounded in local court-anchor progress and OCR-readiness artifacts updated on 2026-06-07.

authority_profile: Medium-to-high authority for what a public body disclosed or procured; weak for deployment impact, legality, or efficacy unless corroborated.
access_method: Official registers, procurement portals, tender notices, award pages, contract pages, and public-body project pages.
reuse_constraints: Metadata-first extraction; do not infer deployment, legality, or AI capability beyond source text.
jurisdiction_scope: Country, regional, and municipal public-sector jurisdictions.
update_cadence: Periodic / batch releases varying by portal.
likely_case_types: Algorithm registers, procurement anomalies, AI system disclosures, and public deployment surfaces.
saturation_metric: 2 municipal public AI register rows, 1 Slovenian official police project-page row, 2 UK regulator-document anchors, and 1 Helsinki buyer-side procurement companion with IBM-consistent award metadata but no exact row-specific linkage yet.
local_seed_evidence: The shared package supports a bounded disclosure-surface comparison across register, project-page, and regulator-document classes, plus a Helsinki buyer-side procurement middle state below exact contract proof.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for disclosure-surface heterogeneity and public-body-existence claims; still weak for buyer-side traceability, capability, legality, or impact claims.
next_verification_move: Add one buyer-side tender, award, contract, or public impact-assessment companion for either register row, or keep the family bounded at register-plus-non-register disclosure surfaces.
dashboard_hook: Jurisdiction/language map; disclosure-field completeness chart; register-versus-project-page-versus-regulator panel; Route-B blocker panel.
provenance_note: Grounded in the shared disclosure-surface package plus the Amsterdam/Helsinki/UK/ORION route-control artifacts updated on 2026-06-07; repeated same-shell procurement probing should not be treated as progress.

authority_profile: Primary only for what the issuer disclosed; self-interested and incomplete for broader truth claims.
access_method: Public status pages, transparency reports, release notes, product incident notes, GitHub advisories/issues.
reuse_constraints: Public notices only; no private support content or hidden dashboards.
language_scope: Mostly English with some localized notices.
likely_case_types: Outages, rollbacks, remediation notices, policy changes, and deployment pauses.
saturation_metric: 5 metadata-normalized issuer/coordinator notice rows exist locally for Auto-GPT, Langflow, Claude Code, and CrewAI-linked cases.
local_seed_evidence: Dedicated issuer/coordinator subledger separates formal advisories, repo issues, and coordination notices for current security rows.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Useful for timing and remediation claims; should rarely stand alone for incident characterization.
next_verification_move: Verify missing direct vendor remediation details for Langflow and CrewAI while preserving the advisory-vs-issue distinction.
dashboard_hook: Remediation timeline overlay; issuer-vs-independent-source comparison table.
provenance_note: Grounded in public GitHub advisories/issues, release-note metadata, and CERT/CC-linked coordination evidence already normalized locally.

authority_profile: Variable authority; strongest when tied to CERTs, established labs, coordinated disclosure, or peer-reviewed work.
access_method: Public blogs, conference papers, CERT notes, vendor advisories, and coordinated-disclosure pages.
reuse_constraints: Preserve bounded metadata and defensive lessons only; exclude exploit payloads and dangerous reproduction detail.
language_scope: Mostly English with some multilingual advisories.
likely_case_types: Prompt injection, exfiltration, model inversion, unsafe agent tooling, red-team findings, and coordinated disclosures.
saturation_metric: 9-row local security package separates vendor advisories, one repo-issue-only notice surface, CERT/CC coordination rows, vendor release-note signals, and independent-lab public-security exemplars.
local_seed_evidence: Dedicated local security artifacts distinguish vendor-authored advisories, repo-issue-only notice depth, CERT/CC coordination, release-note remediation signals, explicit zero-state closure thresholds, reviewer-panel counts, and independent-lab exemplars without collapsing them into one authority class.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Useful for mechanism discovery, source-independence comparison, coordination posture, and remediation-visibility comparisons when vendor/CERT/release-note/independent-lab roles stay explicit; still weak for prevalence or complete remediation claims.
next_verification_move: First remove one zero state (`supply_chain_or_plugin_extension` or `peer_reviewed_security_case_support`), then add one peer-reviewed exemplar or similarly strong supply-chain, plugin-extension, or identity-abuse row; do not spend the next cycle on more Langflow/CrewAI notice-depth polishing alone.
dashboard_hook: Security-source authority matrix; advisory-to-CVE linkage graph; remediation-visibility overlay; Route-B security hardening panel.
provenance_note: Grounded in the local security artifacts updated via the current shard-004 publication-routing, zero-state, and reviewer-panel packages.

authority_profile: Secondary corroborative authority valuable for discovery, local context, and timeline stitching, but weaker than official sources.
access_method: Public news sites, investigative outlets, archives, and broadcaster pages.
reuse_constraints: Use quotations sparingly; preserve outlet/date/URL and do not treat visibility as prevalence.
language_scope: Multilingual, including underrepresented and local-language reporting.
likely_case_types: Discovery leads, local context, public reactions, timeline stitching, and cases absent from English summaries.
saturation_metric: 6 local leads across Finland, Lithuania, Slovenia, and Estonia; 1 lead already upgraded from media/discovery friction to a direct official source.
local_seed_evidence: Outlet-authority matrix exists and the queue shows one completed lead-to-authority upgrade path via the Slovenian ORION case.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for discovery, coverage-gap analysis, and rare-language methods; still weak alone for strong legal or normative claims.
next_verification_move: Resolve one additional direct source from Finland, Lithuania, or Estonia and record whether the stronger authority comes from broadcaster, police, court, or agency sources.
dashboard_hook: Jurisdiction/language map; lead-to-authority upgrade funnel; rare-language methods note.
provenance_note: Grounded in the local rare-language queue plus the outlet-authority matrix and source-resolution log; translation alone is insufficient for final legal/policy claims.

authority_profile: Medium-to-high authority for retractions, correction notices, benchmark-integrity notes, and scholarly dispute records depending on source.
access_method: Public journal notices, publisher pages, DOI registries, retraction databases, and institutional reports.
reuse_constraints: Public/citation-ready sources preferred; preserve publisher-specific reuse terms.
language_scope: Mostly English with some multilingual notices.
likely_case_types: Retracted AI papers, benchmark contamination, fabricated evaluation claims, and research-integrity anomalies.
saturation_metric: 7 locally classified rows split into 4 evidence-depth classes: 1 direct publisher-notice-text exemplar, 1 publisher-visible retracted-status row, 2 DOI-pair or mediated-editor rows, and 3 weak specialist/platform-gated rows.
local_seed_evidence: Shard-008 plus the source-map inventory support a bounded research-integrity family with explicit source-depth classes rather than one undifferentiated bucket.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Strong for bounded claims about article identification, retraction/correction surfaces, publisher-notice depth, and source-resolution friction; weaker for editorial reasoning where direct notice text is blocked or absent.
next_verification_move: Probe one blocked or mediated row for direct publisher notice text while preserving the four current family-depth classes and keeping weak specialist/platform-gated rows explicitly below direct-notice grade.
dashboard_hook: Research-integrity dashboard facet; publisher-notice upgrade funnel; scholarly-integrity timeline; Route-A limitations panel.
provenance_note: Grounded in 2026-06-07 shard-008 DOI/publisher verification rows plus the published four-class family inventory.

reuse_constraints: Avoid humiliation, doxxing, or sensationalism; preserve only public-safe metadata.
likely_case_types: AI persona incidents, fake-AI service claims, uncanny deployments, and governance-threshold anomalies.
saturation_metric: Conceptually important for LIMEN novelty, but no dedicated family inventory exists yet.
local_seed_evidence: Current support is indirect via taxonomy and residual-category work rather than a dedicated source-family package.
evidence_strength_for_claims: Potentially important for article differentiation if sharply bounded and reviewer-legible.
next_verification_move: Build a residual-category intake note with exclusion rules and evidentiary thresholds before adding many cases.
